title: Berrettini on RG Comeback: I’m A Bit of a Psycho
description: Former quarterfinalist Matteo Berrettini reacts after scoring his first win in Paris in 5 years.

title: Monfils: “Roland-Garros gave me 99.9% of my dreams, except the trophy”
description: Gaël Monfils, on the eve of his final Roland-Garros campaign, reveals the tournament has fulfilled 99.9% of his dreams. The only missing piece? The trophy. He prepares for an emotional night-session opener against Hugo Gaston, reflecting on his career and impact.

title: De Jong ends Wawrinka’s emotional Roland-Garros farewell on Court Simonne-Mathieu
description: Jesper de Jong ended Stan Wawrinka's Roland-Garros story on Court Simonne-Mathieu (6-3, 3-6, 6-3, 6-4). The 41-year-old Swiss, playing the tournament for the 21st and final time, was honoured afterwards in a video tribute.

title: Swiatek on Lessons Learned from Champs in Roland Garros Quest
description: "It's harder to stay on the top, because you need to adjust your game," Iga Swiatek said.

title: Carreño Busta beats Lehecka in straight sets, the third seed out of the men’s draw in two days
description: Pablo Carreño Busta, a former world No. 10 who was ranked No. 876 less than two years ago, beat 12th seed Jiří Lehečka 6-3, 7-6(3), 6-3 – his first top-20 win in two years and the third men's seed out in 48 hours.

title: Samsonova becomes the second women’s seed out at Roland-Garros, beaten by Teichmann in straight sets
description: Jil Teichmann defeated Liudmila Samsonova 6-4, 6-4 on Monday. She'll face Pole Magdalena Frech in the next round

title: Djokovic sets men’s Slam appearance record and survives Mpetshi Perricard in four
description: Novak Djokovic, two days after turning 39 and on his record 82nd Grand Slam appearance, beat Frenchman Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard 5-7, 7-5, 6-1, 6-4 on Court Philippe-Chatrier to open Roland-Garros – his 22nd consecutive appearance at the tournament, tying Richard Gasquet's record.

title: Cirstea outlasts Efremova in Roland-Garros farewell opener
description: In a generational clash at Roland-Garros, veteran Sorana Cirstea, playing her final tournament in Paris, overcame 17-year-old French wild card Ksenia Efremova. The Romanian secured a 6-3, 6-1 victory, marking her first main-draw win here since 2022 and equalling a career-best on clay.

description: Nishesh Basavareddy, a 21-year-old American wild card and the world No. 156, beat seventh seed Taylor Fritz 7-6(5), 7-6(5), 6-7(9), 6-1 on his Roland-Garros debut. The main surprise of Day 1.

title: Fonseca beats French qualifier Pavlovic, lines up Prizmic in a second-round meeting of bright young talents
description: João Fonseca (No 28) beat French qualifier Luka Pavlovic 7-6(6), 6-4, 6-2 on Court Simonne-Mathieu, in front of a crowd that felt closer to Rio than to Paris.

title: Tommy Paul, more seriously than Coco Gauff a week ago, would cancel the loser’s speech after a final if he could

title: Tearful Triumph: Anguished Kostyuk Wins RG Opener After Missile Attack Near Home
description: "If [the missile] was 100 meters closer, I probably wouldn't have a mom and a sister today," Marta Kostyuk said of missile attack near home.

title: Qualified Cinà beats Opelka to win his first Grand Slam match in five on debut
description: Federico Cina beat Reilly Opelka 3-6, 6-4, 6-2, 6-7 (6), 6-4 on Sunday afternoon and will play the winner of the match between Swiss Stan Wawrinka and Dutchman Jesper de Jong in the next round

title: Andreeva returns to Chatrier without trouble, opens Roland-Garros with a routine win
description: Mirra Andreeva (No 8), the highest-ranked woman in action on the opening day of Roland-Garros, returned to Court Philippe-Chatrier – the scene of her quarter-final exit a year ago to French wild card Loïs Boisson – and beat France's Fiona Ferro 6-3, 6-3 to open the tournament.

title: Etcheverry becomes the first seed out at Roland-Garros, losing in straight sets to Borges
description: The first seed out at Roland-Garros 2026 was Tomás Martín Etcheverry (No 23) – and Nuno Borges did not need a tight set to do it. The Portuguese beat the Argentine 6-3, 6-4, 6-2.

title: Kostyuk opens Roland-Garros with emotional win dedicated to Ukraine
description: A missile destroyed a building 100 metres from her parents' home in Kyiv on Sunday morning. Hours later, Marta Kostyuk (No 15) walked onto Court Simonne-Mathieu and beat Oksana Selekhmeteva 6-2, 6-3.

title: Italian Open 2026 Day Nine Recap
